title: Bitter Fluffy Malaysian Pappardelle Pasta Noodles
description: A unique and flavorful one-pot dish featuring pappardelle pasta, KFC-style double-fried chicken, and Ayurvedic rajasic-friendly ingredients.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add 2 inches of galangal and 2 stalks of lemongrass and sauté for 2 minutes.
Add 4 cups of chicken broth, 1 cup of coconut milk, 1 teaspoon of fish sauce, 1 teaspoon of soy sauce, and 1 teaspoon of sugar.
Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Add 8 oz of pappardelle pasta and cook for 8-10 minutes, or until al dente.
Add 2 cups of water and continue to simmer for another 2-3 minutes.
Stir in 2 tablespoons of toasted sesame oil, 1 teaspoon of turmeric, 1 teaspoon of cumin, 1 teaspoon of coriander, and 1 teaspoon of salt.
Add 4 breasts of double-fried chicken and stir to combine.
Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until the chicken is fully coated in the sauce.
Garnish with 1 cup of toasted peanuts, 1 cup of scallions, and 1 cup of fresh cilantro.
